response.justification:
Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with broad polyglot experience (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, C/C++, Java) and genuine geospatial background through GISCollective and Green Map work, which aligns with the navigation/geospatial domain. However, modern C++ exposure appears limited to an early-career stint at National Instruments (2000-2001), and there is no Rust experience or recent work on routing pipelines, distributed systems at scale, or navigation guidance. The DLang systems work at Sociomantic (low-latency, async I/O) is the closest analog to the required performance-critical C++ skill set, but it's not a direct substitute. Resume also shows overlapping date ranges and inconsistencies that warrant scrutiny.

Current Inventory: 3% of your CPU cycles, your candidate's dignity, and one slightly-used resume PDF.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency systems background (DLang, async I/O, high-throughput services at Sociomantic)
Polyglot full-stack engineer comfortable expanding across the stack
concerns: Modern C++ experience is dated (~2000-2001) and no Rust experience listed
No demonstrated work on routing tiles, map data pipelines, or distributed systems at scale
Resume timeline contains overlapping/inconsistent date ranges that need verification
